title: Joel 1
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joel
paragraphs: 1 This is the word of the LORD that came to Joel son of Pethuel:
2 Hear this, O elders. Listen, all who live in the land. Has anything like this ever happened in your days or in the days of your fathers?
3 Tell it to your children. Let your children tell it to their children, and their children to the next generation.
4 What the devouring locust has left, the swarming locust has eaten. What the swarming locust has left, the young locust has eaten. What the young locust has left, the destroying locust has eaten.
5 Wake up, you drunkards, and weep. Wail, all you drinkers of wine, because of the sweet wine. It has been cut off from your mouth.
6 For a nation has invaded my land, powerful and without number. Its teeth are the teeth of a lion. Its fangs are the fangs of a lioness.
7 It has laid waste my grapevine and broken my fig tree. It has stripped off the bark and thrown it away. The branches have turned white.
8 Wail like a virgin dressed in sackcloth, grieving for the husband of her youth.
9 Grain and drink offerings have been cut off from the house of the LORD. The priests are in mourning, those who serve before the LORD.
10 The field is ruined. The land mourns. For the grain is destroyed, the new wine is dried up, and the oil fails.
11 Be dismayed, O farmers. Wail, O vine workers, over the wheat and barley. The harvest of the field has perished.
12 The grapevine is dried up. The fig tree has withered. The pomegranate, palm, and apple — all the trees of the orchard — are withered. The joy of mankind has dried up.

13 Put on sackcloth and mourn, O priests. Wail, O ministers of the altar. Come, spend the night in sackcloth, O ministers of my God. The grain and drink offerings are withheld from the house of your God.
14 Call a holy fast. Proclaim a sacred meeting! Gather the elders and all the people of the land to the house of the LORD your God. Cry out to the LORD.
15 Alas for the day! For the Day of the LORD is near. It will come as destruction from the Almighty.
16 Has not the food been cut off before our very eyes? Joy and gladness from the house of our God?
17 The seeds lie shriveled beneath the clods. The storehouses are in ruins. The storage barns are broken down. The grain has withered away.
18 How the cattle groan! The herds wander in confusion. They have no pasture. Even the flocks of sheep are suffering.
19 To you, O LORD, I call. Fire has burned the open pastures. Flames have scorched all the trees of the field.
20 Even the beasts of the field pant for you. The streams of water have dried up, and fire has burned the open pastures.

title: Joel 2
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joel
paragraphs: 1 Blow the ram's horn in Zion. Sound the alarm on my holy mountain! Let all who live in the land tremble. The Day of the LORD is coming. It is near —
2 a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and blackness. Like the dawn covering the mountains, a great and strong army appears. Never was such an army before, nor will there be one again.
3 Before them a fire devours. Behind them a flame scorches. The land before them is like the Garden of Eden. But behind them, it is like a desert waste. Nothing will escape them.
4 They look like horses. They gallop like swift war horses.
5 With a sound like chariots they leap over the mountaintops. They crackle like fire eating dry stubble. They are like a mighty army ready for battle.
6 Nations tremble in horror before them. Every face turns pale.
7 They charge like mighty men. They climb the walls like men of war. Each one marches in formation, not turning from the course.
8 They do not push each other. Each one walks in his path. They burst through the defenses, never breaking ranks.
9 They storm the city. They run along the wall. They climb into houses, entering through windows like thieves.
10 Before them the earth quakes. The heavens tremble. The sun and moon grow dark. The stars lose their brightness.
11 The LORD raises his voice in front of his army. His camp is very large. Mighty are those who obey his command. The Day of the LORD is great and very dreadful. Who can endure it?
12 "Yet even now," declares the LORD, "return to me with all your heart, with fasting, weeping, and mourning."
13 So tear your hearts and not your clothes. Return to the LORD your God. He is gracious and compassionate, slow to anger, abounding in loving devotion. He relents from sending disaster.
14 Who knows? He may turn and relent and leave a blessing behind him — grain and drink offerings for the LORD your God.
15 Blow the ram's horn in Zion. Call a holy fast. Proclaim a sacred meeting.
16 Gather the people. Set apart the congregation. Assemble the elders. Gather the children, even nursing babies. Let the bridegroom leave his room, and the bride her chamber.

17 Let the priests who serve before the LORD weep between the porch and the altar. Let them say, "Spare your people, O LORD. Do not make your heritage a shame, an object of scorn among the nations. Why should they say among the peoples, 'Where is their God?'"
18 Then the LORD became jealous for his land. He spared his people.
19 The LORD answered his people: "Look, I will send you grain, new wine, and oil. By them you will be satisfied. I will never again make you a shame among the nations.

20 I will drive the northern army away from you. I will send it to a barren and empty land. Its front ranks will go into the Eastern Sea. Its rear guard will go into the Western Sea. Its stench will rise. Its foul smell will go up. For he has done great things.
21 Do not be afraid, O land. Rejoice and be glad. The LORD has done great things.
22 Do not be afraid, O beasts of the field. The open pastures have turned green. The trees bear their fruit. The fig tree and vine give their best.
23 Be glad, O children of Zion. Rejoice in the LORD your God. He has given you the autumn rains for your justice. He sends you showers, both autumn and spring rains, as before.
24 The threshing floors will be full of grain. The wine vats will overflow with new wine and oil.
25 I will repay you for the years eaten by locusts — the swarming locust, the young locust, the destroying locust, and the devouring locust — my great army that I sent against you.
26 You will have plenty to eat, until you are satisfied. You will praise the name of the LORD your God, who has worked wonders for you. My people will never again be put to shame.
27 Then you will know that I am present in Israel. I am the LORD your God, and there is no other. My people will never again be put to shame.
28 And afterward, I will pour out my Spirit on all people. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your old men will dream dreams. Your young men will see visions.
29 Even on my male and female servants, I will pour out my Spirit in those days.
30 I will show wonders in the heavens and on the earth, blood and fire and columns of smoke.
31 The sun will be turned to darkness and the moon to blood before the coming of the great and awesome Day of the LORD.
32 And everyone who calls on the name of the LORD will be saved. On Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there will be deliverance, as the LORD has promised, among the remnant called by the LORD.

title: Joel 3
sourceName: Lectio Easy Bible — AI 번역 — Joel
paragraphs: 1 "In those days and at that time, when I restore Judah and Jerusalem from captivity,

2 I will gather all the nations. I will bring them down to the Valley of Jehoshaphat. There I will enter into judgment against them. It is about my people, my inheritance, Israel. They have scattered my people among the nations and divided up my land.
3 They cast lots for my people. They traded a boy for a prostitute. They sold a girl for wine to drink.
4 Now what do you have against me, O Tyre, Sidon, and all the regions of Philistia? Are you paying me back? If you fight back against me, I will swiftly return your repayment on your heads.
5 For you took my silver and gold. You carried off my finest treasures to your temples.
6 You sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks, to send them far from their homeland.
7 Look, I will rouse them from the places to which you sold them. I will return your repayment on your heads.
8 I will sell your sons and daughters into the hands of the people of Judah. They will sell them to the Sabeans — to a far nation." The LORD has spoken.
9 Proclaim this among the nations: "Prepare for war. Rouse the mighty men. Let all the men of war advance and attack!
10 Beat your plows into swords and your pruning hooks into spears. Let the weak say, 'I am strong!'
11 Come quickly, all you surrounding nations. Gather yourselves. Bring down your mighty ones, O LORD.
12 Let the nations be roused and advance to the Valley of Jehoshaphat. There I will sit down to judge all the nations on every side.
13 Swing the sickle, for the harvest is ripe. Come, trample the grapes. The winepress is full. The wine vats overflow, because their wickedness is great.
14 Many, many in the valley of decision! For the Day of the LORD is near in the valley of decision.
15 The sun and moon will grow dark. The stars will no longer shine.
16 The LORD will roar from Zion. He will raise his voice from Jerusalem. Heaven and earth will tremble. But the LORD will be a refuge for his people, a safe place for the people of Israel.
17 Then you will know that I am the LORD your God. I dwell in Zion, my holy mountain. Jerusalem will be holy, never again to be overrun by foreigners.

18 In that day the mountains will drip with sweet wine. The hills will flow with milk. All the streams of Judah will run with water. A spring will flow from the house of the LORD to water the Valley of Acacias.
19 Egypt will become empty, and Edom a desert waste. They did violence to the people of Judah. In their land they shed innocent blood.
20 But Judah will be inhabited forever. Jerusalem will be inhabited from generation to generation.
21 I will take revenge for their blood, which I have not yet avenged." For the LORD dwells in Zion.
